'''Numerals''' in [[Aelaki]] are richly derivational. Each integer is built from a triconsonantal root shaped by '''gender vowels''', '''semantic role''', and agreement with counters.

== Gender Vowels ==

Numeral roots take different vowels depending on the [[Aelaki_plural|gender]] of the noun they modify:

{| class="wikitable"
! Gender !! Vowel pair
|-
| [[Child]] || u / i
|-
| [[Female]] || o / e
|-
| [[Male]] || a / æ
|}

== Six Semantic Roles ==

Every numeral root can be inflected into six distinct semantic roles:

{| class="wikitable"
! Role !! Meaning !! Example (root B-L "two")
|-
| '''Cardinal''' || counting quantity || ''Bal'' "two"
|-
| '''Ordinal''' || position in sequence || ''Kezon'' "second"
|-
| '''Partitive''' || subset of a whole || ''Babal'' "two of"
|-
| '''Fractional''' || division into parts || ''Kalakel'' "half"
|-
| '''Collective''' || all members as a group || ''Bæl'' "both"
|-
| '''Adverbial''' || frequency or repetition || ''Balte'' "twice"
|}

== Negative Series ==

A '''negative series''' is formed by inserting '''/f/''' before C₂ in the root. Each negative role carries a distinct semantic shift:

{| class="wikitable"
! Negative Role !! Meaning !! Example
|-
| Negative cardinal || absent quantity || —
|-
| Negative ordinal || counting backwards || ''Maflon'' "second-last"
|-
| Negative partitive || removal from a set || —
|-
| Negative collective || debt or obligation || —
|-
| Negative adverbial || reversal or undoing || —
|}

== Base System ==

Aelaki numerals are '''originally base 8'''. The first eight numbers have obscure, archaic meanings. Higher numbers are more transparent:

* '''9''' — means "new"
* '''10''' (''b'el'') — means "two hands"
* '''12''' (''wiv'') — means "rings"

=== Mnemonic Rhyme ===

The traditional mnemonic for learning the first few numbers:

: ''"Woman Zero bore Child One; Child grew to Woman Two, then Man Three..."''

This rhyme reflects the gender cycle embedded in the numeral system: the genders rotate through the first few integers (female, child, female, male, ...).

== Agreement ==

Numerals agree with counters and take the appropriate gender vowels of the noun they modify. The /f/-infix for negatives combines freely with all six semantic roles and all gender vowel patterns.

[[Category:Aelaki grammar]]
